# Template Site Blocks

Block docs are generated from `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/*BlockDoc` exports and their matching `*BlockSchema` exports.

## Category Order

1. `hero_intro`
2. `content`
3. `features_services`
4. `process`
5. `trust_proof`
6. `testimonials`
7. `faq`
8. `cta`
9. `contact`
10. `resources`
11. `utility`

## Blocks

### `hero_intro`

#### Hero Simple Centered

- Category: `hero_intro`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/hero-simple-centered.tsx`
- Schema export: `heroSimpleCenteredBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `heroSimpleCenteredBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `heroSimpleCentered`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/hero-simple-centered.png`

Centered text hero for pages that need a clean introduction without a large background image. Use it for overview or about-style pages where the copy should lead.

Content notes:
- Works well with one or two actions beneath the intro copy.
- Only use the optional founder/footer fields when the person, role, and image are real and approved.

#### Hero with Background Overlay

- Category: `hero_intro`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/hero-with-background-overlay.tsx`
- Schema export: `heroWithBackgroundOverlayBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `heroWithBackgroundOverlayBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `heroWithBackgroundOverlay`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/hero-with-bg-overlay.png`

Full-width hero with dark wallpaper styling, background image treatment, announcement badge, headline, subheadline, and a single action. Choose it for a high-impact homepage or landing page opener.

Content notes:
- Use a background image that stays readable under the overlay.
- Best for one primary CTA, not a long button group.
- Verify ratings, review counts, service areas, and licensing claims before publishing.

#### Intro Header

- Category: `hero_intro`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/section-intro-header.tsx`
- Schema export: `sectionIntroHeaderBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `sectionIntroHeaderBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `sectionIntroHeader`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/section-intro-header.png`

Standalone section header with eyebrow, highlighted heading, and short intro copy. Use it to introduce a page section or a simple page header when no hero image or CTA is needed.

Content notes:
- Keep intro copy short; this block is a header, not a long prose section.
- Use the large size for page-level intros and default size for section intros.

#### Wallpaper Hero with Image

- Category: `hero_intro`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/wallpaper-hero-with-image.tsx`
- Schema export: `wallpaperHeroWithImageBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `wallpaperHeroWithImageBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `wallpaperHeroWithImage`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/wallpaper-hero-with-image.png`

Service-page hero with a large headline, supporting copy, one or more actions, an optional announcement badge, and a prominent right-side image. Choose it when the page needs a strong service-specific opening with visual context.

Content notes:
- Best near the top of a page, before trust markers or deeper service detail.
- Use one primary action and, when helpful, one secondary action.
- Use a real, relevant service image with accurate alt text; avoid unsupported location, licensing, rating, or response-time claims.
- Generate the image as a landscape composition, preferably around a 4:3 aspect ratio. Avoid portrait images for this block; the hero crops better on mobile with landscape artwork, and the same image may be reused in service cards or other landscape-oriented blocks.

### `content`

#### Content Split with Portrait

- Category: `content`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/content-split-with-portrait.tsx`
- Schema export: `contentSplitWithPortraitBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `contentSplitWithPortraitBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `contentSplitWithPortrait`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/content-split-with-portrait.png`

Narrative split section with prose on the left and a portrait-style image area on the right. Use it for founder, team, approach, or credibility storytelling that benefits from a person or crew image.

Content notes:
- Desktop and mobile photos can be authored separately.
- Lists render as checkmarked items.
- Avoid founder, credential, experience, or certification claims unless verified.

#### Content Two Column Simple

- Category: `content`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/content-two-column-simple.tsx`
- Schema export: `contentTwoColumnSimpleBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `contentTwoColumnSimpleBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `contentTwoColumnSimple`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/content-two-column-simple.png`

Two-column prose block for explaining a topic in more detail after an intro header. Use it when copy benefits from side-by-side context, supporting explanation, or short checkmarked lists.

Content notes:
- Both columns support rich text; unordered lists render as checkmarked items.
- Works best with balanced left and right column lengths.

#### Content Two Column With Image

- Category: `content`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/content-two-column-with-image.tsx`
- Schema export: `contentTwoColumnWithImageBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `contentTwoColumnWithImageBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `contentTwoColumnWithImage`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/content-two-column-with-image.png`

Two-column content section with a heading and image on the left and rich text on the right. Use it for story or problem-framing sections where one strong image supports longer copy.

Content notes:
- Use a landscape-oriented image with accurate alt text.
- The right column supports paragraphs, links, lists, and blockquotes.

#### Content Two Column With Right Image

- Category: `content`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/content-two-column-with-right-image.tsx`
- Schema export: `contentTwoColumnWithRightImageBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `contentTwoColumnWithRightImageBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `contentTwoColumnWithRightImage`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/content-two-column-with-right-image.png`

Content section with header copy, rich text, and a right-side image that can be framed or edge-blended. Use it for service explanations, diagnostic detail, or approach sections that need visual support.

Content notes:
- Always produce a prepared RGBA/transparent PNG for this block and set fadeEdges: true. The photo should dissolve softly into the page background, with the subject fully opaque near the center or right side and all outer edges feathered to transparency.
- Choose image orientation from the body copy length: use a landscape image when the rich-text body is less than 130 words; use a portrait image when the body is 130 words or more.
- Avoid hard rectangular crop lines, frames, shadows, or visible background color blocks. Generate the canvas in the chosen orientation and set width and height from the final asset.

### `features_services`

#### Features Cards

- Category: `features_services`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/features-cards.tsx`
- Schema export: `featuresCardsBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `featuresCardsBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `featuresCards`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/featured-cards.png`

Image-card grid for highlighting major services, capabilities, or benefit groups. Use it when each item needs a visual, eyebrow label, title, and short supporting copy.

Content notes:
- Currently used on the landing page for four primary service cards.
- Use consistent image style and accurate alt text across cards.
- Keep card copy concise so card heights stay manageable.

#### Features Icon Grid

- Category: `features_services`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/features-icon-grid.tsx`
- Schema export: `featuresIconGridBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `featuresIconGridBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `featuresIconGrid`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/features-icon-grid.png`

Compact icon grid for service scopes, benefits, standards, or common job types. Use it when visitors need to scan several short items quickly.

Content notes:
- Works especially well with 3, 5, 6, or 8 items.
- Keep each item to a short headline and one concise description.
- Verify credentials, availability, ratings, and warranty claims before using them as feature copy.

#### Features Two Column

- Category: `features_services`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/features-two-column.tsx`
- Schema export: `featuresTwoColumnBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `featuresTwoColumnBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `featuresTwoColumn`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/features-two-column.png`

Two-column feature list with larger icon treatments and room for fuller explanations. Use it when each benefit needs more persuasive detail than the compact icon grid allows.

Content notes:
- Items are displayed in rows of two on large screens.
- Best with an even number of features, commonly four.
- Keep descriptions factual and avoid unverified guarantees or service claims.

#### Services Stacked Alternating With Images

- Category: `features_services`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/services-stacked-alternating-with-images.tsx`
- Schema export: `servicesStackedAlternatingWithImagesBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `servicesStackedAlternatingWithImagesBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `servicesStackedAlternatingWithImages`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/services-stacked-alternating-with-images.png`

Stacked service list with alternating text and image layouts plus optional links. Use it for service index pages where each service deserves a short explanation and route to a detail page.

Content notes:
- Works well with 4-6 services.
- Each service can include a Learn more link to a detail page.
- Use relevant service images where available; fallback images repeat by position.

### `process`

#### Steps

- Category: `process`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/steps.tsx`
- Schema export: `stepsBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `stepsBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `steps`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/steps.png`

Step-by-step process block for explaining how hiring, scheduling, or project delivery works. Use it when clarifying the customer journey will reduce friction before contact.

Content notes:
- Steps are automatically numbered by position.
- Best with 3-6 steps; the current service pages use four.
- Avoid promising timing, pricing, or warranty details unless they are accurate for the client.

### `trust_proof`

#### Stats Minimal Centered

- Category: `trust_proof`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/stats-minimal-centered.tsx`
- Schema export: `statsMinimalCenteredBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `statsMinimalCenteredBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `statsMinimalCentered`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/stats-minimal-centered.png`

Centered stats band for a small set of credibility metrics. Use it when the client has verified numbers that help establish scale, experience, or availability.

Content notes:
- Best with 3-4 stats, though the layout supports up to six.
- Only use metrics that can be substantiated, such as years in business, jobs completed, ratings, or response coverage.

#### Trust Bar

- Category: `trust_proof`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/trust-bar.tsx`
- Schema export: `trustBarBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `trustBarBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `trustBar`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/trustbar.png`

Compact horizontal trust-marker strip with icons and short text. Use it near the top of a page or between major sections to reinforce credibility without interrupting the flow.

Content notes:
- Supports 1-5 columns; current pages commonly use four or five items.
- Keep each item short enough to scan in one line or two.
- Verify licensing, insurance, review, warranty, and emergency-service claims before publishing.

### `testimonials`

#### Testimonial With Large Quote

- Category: `testimonials`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/testimonial-with-large-quote.tsx`
- Schema export: `testimonialWithLargeQuoteBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `testimonialWithLargeQuoteBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `testimonialWithLargeQuote`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/testimonial-with-large-quote.png`

Single large testimonial quote with optional avatar, name, and byline. Use it as a focused proof point between content sections or near the end of a service page.

Content notes:
- A single paragraph quote works best.
- Use real customer quotes only with permission or approved source material.
- Name, byline, rating context, and image alt text should not imply facts that are not verified.

#### Testimonials Animated Three Column Grid

- Category: `testimonials`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/testimonials-animated-three-column-grid.tsx`
- Schema export: `testimonialsAnimatedThreeColumnGridBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `testimonialsAnimatedThreeColumnGridBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `testimonialsAnimatedThreeColumnGrid`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/testimonials-animated-three-column-grid.png`

Animated multi-column testimonial grid for showing a larger body of customer reviews. Use it on landing pages or high-proof pages where several short reviews are available.

Content notes:
- Add at least 6 testimonials so the animated grid stays balanced.
- The current landing page uses 9 testimonials.
- Ratings default to 5 when omitted, so author real ratings deliberately.
- Use only verified or approved testimonial content.

### `faq`

#### FAQs Two Column Accordion

- Category: `faq`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/faqs-two-column-accordion.tsx`
- Schema export: `faqsTwoColumnAccordionBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `faqsTwoColumnAccordionBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `faqsTwoColumnAccordion`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/faqs-two-column-accordion.png`

Accordion FAQ section with header copy and expandable answers, optionally with answer videos. Use it for common buying, scheduling, pricing, warranty, or service-scope questions.

Content notes:
- FAQ items require both a question and answer to render.
- Answers support paragraphs, lists, and links.
- Optional videos require an MP4 source and should be used sparingly.
- Avoid legal, warranty, financing, or emergency-response claims unless confirmed for the client.

### `cta`

#### Call To Action Simple

- Category: `cta`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/call-to-action-simple.tsx`
- Schema export: `callToActionSimpleBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `callToActionSimpleBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `callToActionSimple`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/call-to-action-simple.png`

Reusable CTA band with highlighted heading, supporting copy, primary and secondary actions, and optional detail columns. Use it near the end of pages to drive estimates, calls, or contact actions.

Content notes:
- Best with one primary action and one secondary action.
- Bottom details support line breaks and are often used for addresses or hours.
- Use empty details when repeated address or hours content would be distracting.

### `contact`

#### Contact Info With Form

- Category: `contact`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/contact-info-with-form.tsx`
- Schema export: `contactInfoWithFormBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `contactInfoWithFormBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `contactInfoWithForm`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/contact-info-with-form.png`

Contact section that combines an embedded contact form with locations, phone/email, and social links from site config. Use it on contact pages rather than duplicating contact details in custom content.

Content notes:
- Form URL, locations, phone, email, and social links come from site config.
- Block fields only control headings and intro text around configured contact data.

### `resources`

#### Latest Blog Posts

- Category: `resources`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/latest-blog-posts.tsx`
- Schema export: `latestBlogPostsBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `latestBlogPostsBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `latestBlogPosts`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/latest-blog-posts.png`

Latest-posts section that fetches and displays the three most recent blog posts with an optional header and archive link. Use it when a page should surface current resources without manually selecting posts.

Content notes:
- Always shows up to 3 posts sorted by post date.
- If there are no posts, the block renders an empty-state message.
- Use the action link for the blog or resource index.

### `utility`

#### Section Divider

- Category: `utility`
- Source: `apps/template-site/src/components/blocks/section-divider.tsx`
- Schema export: `sectionDividerBlockSchema`
- Doc export: `sectionDividerBlockDoc`
- Schema name: `sectionDivider`
- Preview image: `/block-previews/section-divider.png`

Visual spacing and divider block used to separate adjacent content sections. Use it when page rhythm needs a deliberate break between reusable blocks.

Content notes:
- Supports default and compact spacing.
- Do not use it as content; it only controls separation and rhythm.
